title: Animal Caretakers
description: Feed, water, groom, bathe, exercise, or otherwise provide care to promote and maintain the well-being of pets and other animals that are not raised for consumption, such as dogs, cats, race horses, ornamental fish or birds, zoo animals, and mice. Work in settings such as kennels, animal shelters, zoos, circuses, and aquariums. May keep records of feedings, treatments, and animals received or discharged. May clean, disinfect, and repair cages, pens, or fish tanks.

tasks.text: Feed and water animals according to schedules and feeding instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide treatment to sick or injured animals, or contact veterinarians to secure treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine and observe animals to detect signs of illness, disease, or injury.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mix food, liquid formulas, medications, or food supplements according to instructions, prescriptions, and knowledge of animal species.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Do facility laundry and clean, organize, maintain, and disinfect animal quarters, such as pens and stables, and equipment, such as saddles and bridles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Exercise animals to maintain their physical and mental health.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and record animal information, such as weight, size, physical condition, treatments received, medications given, and food intake.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Respond to questions from patrons, and provide information about animals, such as behavior, habitat, breeding habits, or facility activities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer telephones and schedule appointments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise pet owners on how to care for their pets' health.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform animal grooming duties, such as washing, brushing, clipping, and trimming coats, cutting nails, and cleaning ears.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe and caution children petting and feeding animals in designated areas to ensure the safety of humans and animals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean and disinfect surgical equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Find homes for stray or unwanted animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Discuss with clients their pets' grooming needs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Transfer animals between enclosures to facilitate breeding, birthing, shipping, or rearrangement of exhibits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust controls to regulate specified temperature and humidity of animal quarters, nurseries, or exhibit areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Anesthetize and inoculate animals, according to instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, maintain, and repair animal care facility equipment, such as infrared lights, feeding devices, and cages.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train animals to perform certain tasks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order, unload, and store feed and supplies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sell pet food and supplies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
